exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.tip: Drive your feet into the floor and squeeze your shoulder blades together. Lower the bar to your lower chest.
formTips: Keep your chest up and shoulders retracted
Touch the bar to your lower chest — don't bounce
Drive the bar in a slight arc toward your face at the top

exercises.name: Overhead Press
exercises.tip: Press straight up, keeping your core braced. Don't lean excessively back.
formTips: Brace your core hard before each rep
Keep the bar close to your face on the way up
Full lockout at the top

exercises.name: Incline Dumbbell Press
exercises.tip: Set the bench to 30-45 degrees. Lower slowly and get a good chest stretch at the bottom.
formTips: Lower to a full stretch — elbows just below bench level
Don't flare elbows too wide
Squeeze at the top of every rep

exercises.name: Cable Lateral Raise
exercises.tip: Lead with your elbow, not your hand. Slight forward lean keeps the cable pulling at the right angle.
formTips: Lead with elbow, hand slightly below elbow
Control the descent — don't let it swing back
Raise to shoulder height only

exercises.name: Tricep Pushdown
exercises.tip: Keep your elbows pinned at your sides. Full extension at the bottom.
formTips: Lock your elbows in at your sides throughout
Full extension on every rep
Squeeze at the bottom before releasing

exercises.name: Pull-Up
exercises.tip: Start from a full dead hang. Pull your chest to the bar, leading with your elbows.
formTips: Full dead hang at the bottom — no half reps
Pull your elbows down and back, not just up
Chin clearly over the bar at the top

exercises.name: Barbell Bent-Over Row
exercises.tip: Hinge at the hips, chest up. Pull the bar to your lower chest/upper abdomen.
formTips: Keep your back flat — no rounding
Pull the bar into your belly button, not your chest
Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the top

exercises.name: Cable Seated Row
exercises.tip: Sit tall. Pull the handle to your lower chest and squeeze your back hard before releasing.
formTips: Don't round your lower back at the start
Drive your elbows behind your body
Hold the peak contraction for a beat

exercises.name: Face Pull
exercises.tip: Pull to your nose/forehead, with elbows high and flared. Great for shoulder health.
formTips: Elbows must be at or above shoulder height
Pull to your face, not your neck
External rotate at the end — hands behind ears

exercises.name: Dumbbell Curl
exercises.tip: Supinate your wrist as you curl. Keep your upper arm stationary.
formTips: Pin your elbows at your sides
Supinate (turn palm up) as you curl
Full extension at the bottom — don't cut the range short

exercises.name: Barbell Back Squat
exercises.tip: Feet shoulder-width, toes slightly out. Squat to parallel or below. Chest up throughout.
formTips: Take a big belly breath and brace before each rep
Push your knees out in line with your toes
Break parallel — crease of hip below the top of knee

exercises.name: Romanian Deadlift
exercises.tip: Push your hips back, keeping the bar close to your legs. Feel the hamstring stretch before driving back up.
formTips: Bar stays in contact with your legs the whole way down
Soft bend in the knee — this isn't a stiff-leg deadlift
Stop when you feel a strong hamstring stretch

exercises.name: Leg Press
exercises.tip: Place feet shoulder-width in the middle of the platform. Lower until your knees are at 90 degrees.
formTips: Don't lock your knees at the top
Keep your lower back flat against the pad
Control the descent — 2-3 seconds down

exercises.name: Leg Curl
exercises.tip: Curl your heels toward your glutes. Don't let your hips rise off the pad.
formTips: Plant your hips firmly — don't let them rise
Full range of motion — heels toward glutes
Squeeze at the top before lowering

exercises.name: Standing Calf Raise
exercises.tip: Full range: deep stretch at the bottom, full rise at the top. Slow and controlled.
formTips: Get a deep stretch at the bottom — don't bounce
Rise all the way onto your toes
Pause at the top and bottom
